steps.title: List all data values by expanding frequencies
steps.content: From the frequency table, the data set contains: value 13 appearing 8 times, value 20 appearing 1 time, value 27 appearing 8 times, and value 34 appearing 6 times. The total count is $8 + 1 + 8 + 6 = 23$ data points.

steps.title: Arrange in order and identify the median position
steps.content:
With 23 data points, the median is at position $\frac{23 + 1}{2} = 12$. Listing all values in order:
  • Positions 1–8: 13
  • Position 9: 20
  • Positions 10–17: 27
  • Positions 18–23: 34
Position 12 falls in the group of 27s, so the median value is 27.

steps.title: Note on answer choices
steps.content: Based on the frequency table shown, the median of the data set is 27. However, 27 does not appear in the answer choices (A: 1, B: 8, C: 13, D: 20). There may be a discrepancy between the problem data and the provided options. If this problem is from an official source, please verify the table and answer choices.
